AAPL.rationale: PE has compressed from 41x to 32x over six months, partially correcting the overvaluation thesis, but earnings yield of 3.15% still trails risk-free ~4.3% and revenue growth remains ~5% — the risk/reward has improved from clearly unfavorable to only mildly unfavorable.

Earnings yield 3.15% remains ~115bp below risk-free rate; investors still paying a premium for growth Apple isn't delivering on the top line
Margins expanding (gross 46.6% vs 45.0% YoY, operating 31.8% vs 30.8%) and buybacks shrinking share count — fundamental quality improving even as revenue growth stays modest
Tariff exposure (25-55% on China-assembled iPhones) and AI narrative weakness (Siri lawsuit, 'dead money' label) create ongoing uncertainty that caps multiple expansion

AAPL.final_explanation: AAPL's valuation has meaningfully corrected (PE 41x→32x) but remains premium for its growth profile; the bear case has weakened from clearly unfavorable to mildly unfavorable as margins expand and tariff fears partially subside.

CRM.final_explanation: CRM's margin expansion story remains intact and accelerating (19.8% Q1 FY26, guided 21.6% FY26), while valuation has compressed to the cheapest trailing P/E in 2+ years at 43x, creating a favorable risk/reward even with decelerating 7-8% top-line growth.

CSCO.final_explanation: Market has re-rated CSCO aggressively on early recovery signals, but the premium valuation (28x PE) leaves little margin of safety for what remains a modest-growth, margin-compressed business still digesting a large acquisition.

DIS.final_explanation: Disney's turnaround thesis has fully played out — streaming is profitable, margins are at record levels, and the stock is actually cheaper on an earnings basis (23.8x P/E) than it was three months ago at a lower price (28.6x at $98.70), as fundamental improvement has outpaced price appreciation.

GS.final_explanation: GS tariff-selloff thesis from April fully realized (+30%); stock now at fair value with limited further risk-adjusted upside at 15.4x P/E.

JPM.final_explanation: JPM's exceptional Q1 2025 results and franchise quality are undeniable, but the stock has been rewarded far beyond what earnings growth justifies — the P/E has expanded 50% in 12 months to 15.5x, the richest level in 15+ years, making risk-reward unfavorable at new ATHs above consensus price targets.

MCD.final_explanation: US comps deteriorating with no catalyst for recovery; mild negative risk-adjusted reward.

MRK.final_explanation: MRK is a quality pharma compounder de-rated to bargain valuation on Keytruda LOE fears; pipeline catalysts and margin strength support a positive risk-adjusted view at ~8.9x forward P/E.

NKE.final_explanation: NKE is a deep turnaround with severely impaired fundamentals; post-earnings rally has pushed valuation to 23x P/E on depressed TTM, pricing in recovery that remains highly uncertain given tariff headwinds and declining revenue.

NVDA.final_explanation: AI infrastructure capex cycle remains the dominant secular theme; NVDA is the quality leader but valuation has caught up to the story after the April recovery, leaving a mild positive lean rather than a high-conviction buy.

SHW.final_explanation: SHW remains a quality compounder in a challenged cyclical context — premium valuation persists despite flat revenue and housing headwinds, warranting a mild negative lean on risk-adjusted reward.

UNH.final_explanation: UNH is a high-quality healthcare franchise trading at crisis-level valuation (12.5x PE) due to a confluence of negative catalysts — DOJ criminal probe, withdrawn guidance, CEO change. The valuation provides a floor if the situation stabilizes, but the binary legal outcome and earnings uncertainty warrant moderate rather than high conviction.

V.final_explanation: Visa remains a best-in-class compounder with elite margins and tariff-insensitive economics, but fair-to-full valuation and the DOJ antitrust case proceeding past motion-to-dismiss keep risk-adjusted reward in the mild-long territory.
